title: Mid-Century Modern Media Cabinet
summary: Mid-Century Modern media cabinets don’t have to break the bank.
description: Mid-Century Modern media cabinets don’t have to break the bank. Check out how simple it is to build trendy storage on a tight budget! Hint: it only took one sheet of plywood to build!

steps.title: Check Dimensions
steps.body: Check the dimensions of the project to make sure it fits your space.

steps.title: Make Cuts
steps.body: Use the cut list to make your cuts.

steps.title: Create Frame
steps.body: Create your frame by attaching top, bottom, and side pieces with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Add Back Panel
steps.body: Add back panel with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Add Shelf (Optional)
steps.body: Add a shelf to your build with 1 1/4" pocket hole screws & wood glue, if needed. I used mine for a 3D printer, so no shelving was added!

steps.title: Create Handle Cut Outs
steps.body: Use a jig saw to create cut outs for the door handles. The ones on this particular cabinet are 1/2" wide and 3" tall on each door.

steps.title: Attach Doors
steps.body: Use the Kreg cabinet hardware jig to add your full overlay concealed hinges.

steps.title: Add Legs
steps.body: Add your legs as per manufacturer directions. Visit my website at diyhuntress.com for a link to the exact legs used in this build.

steps.title: Sand & Stain
steps.body: Stain your piece! Add artwork to the front to help the unit pop!
